introMarkdown:
During the period from May 4, 2026, to May 11, 2026, a total of four releases were shipped across two repositories, indicating a steady deploy frequency for the projects involved. The most recent release was ci-dokumentor version 0.3.0, which was made available on May 5, 2026. The docker-base-images repository was the busiest, contributing three of the four releases during this timeframe.

Key updates include the release of ci-dokumentor, which has garnered five stars, reflecting its utility and potential for enhancing documentation processes. Additionally, the docker-base-images repository continues to evolve, although it currently holds only one star, indicating room for improvement in user engagement or functionality.

- The release of ci-dokumentor 0.3.0 provides enhanced documentation capabilities, which can streamline onboarding and improve knowledge sharing among platform engineers.
- The docker-base-images repository's three releases suggest ongoing development efforts, which may lead to better base images for containerized applications, thereby improving deployment efficiency for SREs.
- The overall increase in release frequency indicates a commitment to iterative improvements, which is critical for technical contributors looking to adopt and integrate HoverKraft projects into their workflows.
closingMarkdown:
Period: May 4, 2026 - May 11, 2026
Repositories covered: 2
Releases shipped: 4
Busiest project: docker-base-images (3)
Most recent release: ci-dokumentor 0.3.0

Repositories to note:
- ci-dokumentor (1 release, 5 ⭐)
- docker-base-images (3 releases, 1 ⭐)

During this period, the focus was primarily on the docker-base-images repository, which accounted for the majority of releases. The ci-dokumentor also saw a new release, indicating ongoing development and support.

For platform engineers, SREs, and other technical users, the recent activity highlights the importance of staying updated with the latest releases and improvements in the repositories. Engaging with these updates can lead to enhanced performance and compliance in container management and documentation processes.

Next steps:
- Review the release notes for ci-dokumentor 0.3.0 to understand new features and improvements, which may enhance documentation automation.
- Evaluate the latest updates in docker-base-images to benchmark container performance and ensure compliance with current standards.
- Consider adopting the ci-dokumentor for improved documentation practices, which may streamline workflows and enhance team collaboration.

repositories.name: ci-dokumentor
repositories.description: Automated documentation generator for CI/CD components

highlights:
## Release Summary

GitLab support is now available through a new repository provider and CI/CD documentation generator, while GitHub Actions examples now better handle job-level permissions.

Core markdown generation is more reliable for inline code, table code blocks, multiline content, special characters, and overview additional content.

Internal changes include shared section generator refactoring, documentation refreshes, dependency updates, and CI maintenance.

## Release Summary

The actions and workflows documentation has been updated.

Internally, the release workflow now uses the correct permissions and token to create releases reliably.

## Release Summary

Documentation was updated for actions and workflows.

Internal CI workflows now use the correct default test image reference.

## Breaking change(s)

There is no breaking change.

## Release Summary

Image testing has been improved to strengthen validation of published Docker base images.

Internal CI test execution and reporting were refined for image checks.

## Breaking change(s)

There is no breaking change.
